page: Google Cloud Platform m4n-hypermem-64 is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the m4n family. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture and is equipped with 992.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 15.5 GB per core, designed specifically for workloads requiring massive memory footprints relative to compute capacity.

The hardware profile does not include integrated local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource density represents a specific tradeoff, prioritizing massive RAM capacity and dedicated CPU allocation over local storage speed and graphics acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m4n-hypermem-64 offers a high concentration of memory resources per virtual machine, which can reduce the need to scale out to multiple smaller instances for memory-bound tasks. This makes it appropriate for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data-processing pipelines that fit entirely within system memory.
description: Google Cloud Platform m4n-hypermem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the m4n family, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 992.0 GB of RAM. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 15.5 GB per core, this instance is engineered for memory-intensive workloads. It does not include local storage, GPUs, or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring reliance on network-attached storage for data persistence. The high resource density of this instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require massive memory footprints without the overhead of managing multiple smaller nodes. It is designed for large in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
